user_query:
I want to organize our latest CI signal into a lightweight knowledge base and coordinate follow-ups with the team. Use only the files in input/ and produce the outputs under outputs/ as specified below.

Inputs:
- input/build_output.txt (Maven multi-module build/test output)
- input/roster.csv (module,owner,owner_email)
- input/standup_notes.md (notes with Action: lines)
- input/project_brief.yaml (project and release_date)
- input/draft_slack_message.txt (a rough Slack draft that needs rewriting)

Tasks and outputs:
1) Parse build_output.txt and categorize issues by module into one of: compilation, test_failure, style.
   - Module attribution: treat all error/warning lines after a line that starts with "[INFO] Building <module>" or a plugin line ending with "@ <module> ---" as belonging to that <module>, until the next such module marker.
   - Category detection:
     - compilation: lines that indicate compilation problems, e.g., contain "COMPILATION ERROR", "Compilation failure", or Java source paths with "cannot find symbol".
     - test_failure: test failures listed under the Maven Surefire results; count each failure line under the "Failures:" section as one test failure (lines that begin with "[ERROR]   ").
     - style: Checkstyle findings; count each line that begins with "[WARNING]" in the checkstyle phase as one style issue.
   - Produce outputs/issue_summary.json with the following JSON structure:
     {
       "project": string (from project_brief.yaml),
       "release_date": string (from project_brief.yaml),
       "issues": [
         {
           "module": string,
           "category": "compilation"|"test_failure"|"style",
           "issue_count": number,
           "owner_name": string (from roster.csv),
           "owner_email": string (from roster.csv),
           "details": [string, ...]  // brief message excerpts or file/test identifiers per issue
         }, ...
       ]
     }
     - Aggregate by (module, category). For details, include a short string per individual issue, such as a file:line summary for compilation/style or testName:line for test failures.

2) From issue_summary.json, generate outputs/owners_report.csv with header:
   module,category,owner,owner_email,issue_count
   - One row per (module, category) from the JSON. Values must match the JSON aggregation.

3) Build a concise knowledge base document at outputs/knowledge_base.md with sections in this exact order and headings:
   - Project Overview: include project and release_date from project_brief.yaml in one short paragraph.
   - Team Roster: list each module with its owner and owner_email from roster.csv.
   - Current Issues: summarize counts by module and category using issue_summary.json, and include at least one representative detail per module.
   - Action Items: extract every line from standup_notes.md that begins with "Action:" and list them verbatim under this section.

4) Draft an email to the dev team at outputs/team_email.txt. Include a first line starting with "Subject:" that references the project and release_date. In the body, briefly summarize the top issues by module (using issue_summary.json), name the owners (from roster.csv), and clearly ask each owner to address their items by the release_date. Keep the body under 200 words and make it professional and actionable.

5) Rewrite the Slack message in input/draft_slack_message.txt to outputs/draft_slack_message_rewrite.txt with these constraints: under 100 words, friendly and direct tone, include the release_date (from project_brief.yaml), and mention the two modules with the highest total issue_count across categories from issue_summary.json. If there is a tie, sort by module name ascending and pick the first two. Avoid blame and be specific about the ask.

Notes:
- All outputs must be derived from the provided inputs. Do not invent module names or owners.
- The analysis of the command output/error messages in build_output.txt must directly drive the counts and details in issue_summary.json and downstream artifacts.
- The email drafting and Slack rewrite are required deliverables; ensure they reflect the analyzed issues and the release_date.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
